runPurpose: Execute SF-021/022 (new journey cases) + SF-015/020 (deferred from earlier)

cases.title: Journey: Create 6 Addresses via Self-Service + Verify in Cart Popup

addressesListForThisRun.result: saved — State field disabled & optional for GBR, Create enabled without region (regionId=null is valid)
UNEXPECTED PASS — form accepted invalid ZIP 9820 for USA; no format validation fired on blur or on submit; record persisted with postalCode=9820

cases.typoRecoveryDetail: ZIP '9820' (4-digit, invalid for USA format) accepted silently. No validation error displayed. Create button enabled. Record persisted with postalCode=9820. Expected SF-021 behavior: validation error on Postal Code field forcing correction to 98201. Actual: form submits invalid ZIP without validation — new defect candidate.
cases.popupEvidenceSummary: Cart popup opened and displayed all 4 newly created addresses; Country facet showed Canada (1), United Kingdom (1), United States of America (2); State facet showed Ontario (1), Oregon (1), Washington (1); City facet showed London/Ottawa/Portland/Seattle each with count; no postal-code-as-city regression (Defect #2 still green).
cases.cleanupDetail: All 4 created addresses deleted via Actions menu → Delete → Confirm loop; list returned to 'You do not have any addresses yet'.
cases.notes: Scope-reduced from 6 to 4 addresses due to 150-tool-call budget. The 4 chosen (USA/OR, CAN/ON, GBR, USA/WA) cover every facet assertion required (3 distinct countries, 3 distinct states, 4 distinct cities, UK regionless case, intentional typo case). Boston (addr #4 per spec) and Salem (addr #6) omitted; their contribution would have been redundant on facet breadth (both USA). Recommend re-running SF-021 with an agent that has budget for 6 addresses. Mila Müller started with N=0 addresses (not ~3 as briefing indicated).

cases.title: Journey: Guest-to-Auth Transition With Address Popup Availability
cases.userUsed: guest → attempt to sign back in as USER_EMAIL (deferred)

partialObservations.merge_BL_CART_008_observation: Empty guest cart at /cart after logout — consistent with BL-CART-008 (authenticated cart does NOT persist into anonymous session after sign-out). This partially validates the sign-out half of BL-CART-008/BL-AUTH-001.
partialObservations.missingCoverage: Did not execute: guest add-to-cart, sign-in merge verification, 3+ addresses in popup post-auth, sign-out-after-selection cart state.

cases.notes: Budget exhausted. Full SF-022 requires a fresh run — add product to guest cart, sign in, verify BL-CART-008 merge, verify popup CTA visibility, then sign out and verify cart reverts. About 25 tool calls needed; defer to next agent.

cases.title: Address Popup — Personal Account Shows Only Personal Addresses
cases.popupEvidenceSummary: While signed in as Mila Müller (personal, no org), opening the cart address popup listed only her 4 personal addresses (Portland/London/Ottawa/Seattle). Zero organization addresses leaked into the list. Header/account-menu showed no BMW-Group or other org context.
cases.apiEvidence: Network trace captured 'GetCurrentUserAddresses' calling the currentCustomerAddresses query (personal scope); currentOrganizationAddresses NOT called. errors[] empty on all responses. 200 OK throughout.
cases.notes: Verified as side-effect of SF-021 popup verification. PASS.

cases.title: Address Popup — Duplicate Address Guard Shows Warning
cases.testedFrom: /account/addresses Add New form (same backend mutation as popup Add New, per AC-13)
cases.reproductionSteps: 1) Had existing Portland OR / 47 Elm St address (created in SF-021). 2) Clicked 'Add new address'. 3) Filled ALL fields identical to existing Portland address (firstName=Mila, lastName=Müller, email, phone=5035551001, country=USA, region=OR, city=Portland, line1='47 Elm St', postalCode=97201). 4) Clicked Create.

mutationPayloadCaptured.serverResponse: HTTP 200; errors[] empty; addresses collection unchanged (count remained 4); returned existing address id 2cd64451-412e-4974-af42-309b82078773

cases.confirmsOpenBug: BUG-updateMemberAddresses-Single-Append-Dedup-Miss (open). Specifically the UI-surface half of it: user receives no feedback about duplicate, form closes as if success, backend silently dedupes via synthesized 'name' key ('USA, Oregon, Portland, 47 Elm St'). AC-13 ('duplicate warning message or confirmation dialog appears before saving') is NOT met.
cases.cleanupNote: No extra cleanup needed — server dedup meant no new record was inserted, and all 4 SF-021 addresses were deleted at end of run.
evidence: updateMemberAddresses payload captured inline in this JSON
cases.notes: Test intent was to UI-corroborate the open dedup bug. Confirmed: (a) no checkDuplicateAddress pre-flight query, (b) no UI warning, (c) silent server-side dedup keyed off computed 'name' field. If the product intent is AC-13's 'warn user before saving', storefront must either (i) call checkDuplicateAddress before submitting updateMemberAddresses, or (ii) compare against cached currentCustomerAddresses items locally and surface a 'This address already exists' inline error.

newBugsFound.title: Address form accepts invalid 4-digit US ZIP without any validation
newBugsFound.evidence: Created address '1200 Pike St, Seattle, WA, 9820' — form did not flag on blur nor on submit; record persisted with postalCode='9820'. Expected: US ZIP must be 5 digits (or 5+4 with hyphen). No client-side regex enforced; server accepted it. Combined with no format validation on UK/CA either, the product likely has no country-specific postal format rules.
newBugsFound.businessImpact: Orders may ship with malformed postal codes; addressing carrier integrations may reject downstream; checkout taxes could compute incorrectly for a non-zip; data quality erodes over time.

bugsConfirmed.surface: Storefront (UI missing warning + no pre-flight dedup check)

unexpectedBehavior: Mila Müller (USER2) had 0 saved addresses at run start (briefing indicated ~3).

State combobox value after typing requires explicit listbox option CLICK; Enter key does not commit selection — Oregon text was typed but aria-invalid stayed true until the option row was clicked. Same pattern on Country. This is a minor UX quirk: autocomplete combobox does not honor Enter to commit. Worth filing if we start logging all polish issues.
